DDR5-6400.reason: Day 60, 8-source anchors + NEMIX 88 verifications. DDR5 spot $46.500 19th ATH accelerating. SK Hynix diverts HBM4→DDR5 — even highest-margin products cede capacity to DDR5. DDR4 $70 historic break. MLC 6 days. Micron earnings today may trigger next round of price adjustments. This is not a day to wait.

DDR5-5600.reason: Spot $46.500 → tracking ¥9,126 (▲+¥63). 19th ATH acceleration. SK Hynix DDR5 priority strategy. DDR4 $70 break confirms universal bull market. Retail premium narrowed to +15.9%. No reason to wait.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: MLC final 6 days — last week window. Global MLC capacity -41.7% YoY irreversible. NAND Q3 continuing upward (TrendForce). NAND contract Q2 +70-75% QoQ with x4.2-x4.5 cumulative multiplier. Micron earnings today may raise NAND ASP outlook. Brand split 2.5-2.7x structural Day 53. $70.634 DDR4 confirms enterprise demand is extremely strong.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: CDW-G In Stock confirmed at $17,925. CDW consumer price dropped to $17,467 (-2.8%, favorable for buyers, need post-earnings confirmation of sustainability). Newegg OOS Day 17 structural. SS CF Day 54 unverified. Micron earnings may clarify 9550 Pro supply outlook. SK Hynix DDR5 priority may indirectly improve SSD supply. Split buy: CDW-G for qualified buyers, CDW consumer 8-10d channel acceptable at improved pricing.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Mouser 5 units Day 3 — improving trend firmly established. WF6 resolved 6 days. MLC 6 days → partial demand spillover but weaker than dual-contraction scenario. Five-channel replacement coverage healthy. Panic stockpiling unnecessary. Split execution recommended.

gaps.note: Day 60. Enterprise 16GB RDIMM rarely available in public retail channels. Not expected to improve.

NEMIX $3,200 single retail source Day 60. 34th request for tracking column creation. Multiple reference points (HPE $3,665/Dell $4,100/Hynix OEM $3,020-$3,650/A-Tech $2,699) but no single source qualifies for tracking column entry.
ServerSupply Cloudflare Day 54. Primary anchor $17,700 cannot be independently verified.
Memory.net Cloudflare Day 54. Upper bound $3,108 cannot be independently verified.

DiscTech JS offline Day 60 (4 months+6 days). Tracking lag 42-150% structural. No replacement primary source found. CDW/ServerSupply/Newegg alternative coverage is adequate but lacks historical comparability.

muPreMarket.interpretation: Driven by KOSPI Black Tuesday ETF panic spillover + pre-earnings sell-the-news positioning. NOT fundamental Micron deterioration. Polymarket 96% beat probability.
muPreMarket.kospiContext: KOSPI -10% June 23. Samsung -12%, SK Hynix -12%. Trigger: regulatory review of highly-leveraged single-stock ETFs in Korea. Market-wide circuit breaker activated. Memory stocks globally affected by emotional contagion.

relatedDevelopments: SK Hynix 58% HBM market share Q1 2026

kospiBlackTuesday.trigger: South Korean regulatory crackdown on highly-leveraged single-stock ETFs
kospiBlackTuesday.fundamentalsNote: Korea June memory semiconductor exports estimated $38-42B — all-time record. Fundamentals have NEVER been stronger. KOSPI crash is purely regulatory/ETF-driven, zero fundamental memory market deterioration.

nandContractData.mlcStatus: Samsung final shipments June 2026. Kioxia 2D NAND extending to March 15, 2027.

skHynixDDR5Priority.detail: SK Hynix delaying some HBM3E→HBM4 line conversions, redirecting capacity to general-purpose DDR5 DRAM. Reason: DDR5 shortage is so severe and current profitability temporarily exceeds HBM. This is the strongest structural confirmation of the DDR5 bull market in 60 days of tracking — even the highest-margin product lines are ceding capacity to DDR5.

micronAnthropic.detail: Strategic agreement to co-design memory and storage infrastructure for AI data centers. Micron participating in Anthropic Series H funding. Micron integrating Claude into operations. Deepens Micron's AI strategy beyond commodity supply.
